text: For coupled Burgers-swept-KdV with v0=2 sech^2(x+5) and u0 perturbed from m=0 by 0.2v, does the soliton structure survive to T=8.0? Final v should keep single dominant peak with amplitude>=1.0, mass drift<8%, |max|<15.

method: Fourier pseudospectral for all spatial derivatives + explicit RK4 (no dealiasing, no IMEX, no filter)
bank_use_rationale: E1 is the simplest baseline mandated by progressive-complexity discipline. The bank (kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup, kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts) warns this will likely fail. I use Fourier pseudospectral instead of central FD because central FD on v_xxx at Nx=256 is computationally impractical (dt~O(dx^3)). Per discipline rule 6, pseudospectral+explicit RK4 IS the simplest meaningful baseline for a KdV-type equation. No dealiasing, no IMEX yet — these are reserved as E2/E3 escalation choices.

rationale: Explicit RK4 + no dealiasing on the coupled system suffers nonlinear/aliasing-driven explosion at t=0.605, long before T=8. Failure mode = high-wavenumber blow-up — entirely consistent with kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup and kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts. Mass becomes massively negative confirming non-physical aliasing energy in v. Need an implicit/IMEX treatment of v_xxx AND/OR dealiasing. Single-component move: switch to IMEX-CN (CN on v_xxx, explicit on rest).

E1 blew up by t=0.605 due to combined dispersive stiffness and aliasing. Single-component move: replace explicit RK4 with IMEX-CN (treat v_xxx implicitly via CN) on the v equation. Keep everything else (spectral derivatives, no dealiasing). This isolates whether implicit treatment of the dispersion alone restores stability.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + IMEX-CN (CN on v_xxx, explicit forward step on nonlinear/coupling terms for both u and v); single-component upgrade vs E1: time integrator only
bank_use_rationale: Positive: kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass validates IMEX-CN spectral for KdV at A=2. kb-gardner-KdV-method-transfer-moderate-amplitude says IMEX-CN spectral transfers from KdV to Gardner-like dynamics. Negative: kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup rules out IFRK4. kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup warns about amplitude-dependent CFL — at A=2 I pick dt=2e-4 (smaller than KdV pilot's 5e-4, larger than G4's failing 1e-4). Single-component change vs E1: only the time integrator changes; no dealiasing added yet (that is reserved for E3 if needed).

rationale: IMEX-CN stabilized the dispersion (mass of v perfectly preserved at 4.0), confirming kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass: the implicit v_xxx treatment is correct. However, blow-up still occurs at t=0.609 with 126 local maxima at end — classic aliasing-driven nonlinear instability in the quadratic terms (6 v v_x and d/dx(uv)). This is exactly the failure mode flagged by kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts and kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing. The single-component fix is 2/3 dealiasing of the nonlinear products.

F2 isolates aliasing as the remaining failure mode (IMEX-CN handled dispersion; mass conservation perfect). Single-component upgrade: add 2/3 dealiasing rule to all nonlinear product evaluations (v*v_x, u*v products, u*u_x). Bank kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation and kb-gardner-KdV-method-transfer-moderate-amplitude both validate IMEX-CN+2/3 dealiasing as the stable recipe at this amplitude.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + IMEX-CN (CN on v_xxx, explicit forward step on nonlinear/coupling) + 2/3 dealiasing on all nonlinear products; single-component upgrade vs E2: dealiasing only
bank_use_rationale: Positive: kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation is the closest match — IMEX-CN + 2/3 dealiasing was numerically stable for Gardner at A=1.5; we apply the same recipe to the swept-KdV side of the coupled system at A=2. kb-kdv-spectral-solitonAmplitude-conservation confirms IMEX-CN+2/3 conserves amplitude within ~2% over T=2. Negative: kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts and kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing both warn that omitting dealiasing produces spurious peaks — F2 exhibited 126 spurious maxima, validating these. Rejected kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-longTime-dissipation because at T=8 over-diffusive schemes will damp the soliton below the 1.0 threshold. Single-component change vs E2: 2/3 dealiasing mask added; same dt, same time integrator, same spatial discretization.

rationale: Major progress: stable physics through t=2 (|v|max stays near 1.5, mass perfectly conserved at 4.0, single peak). The dealiasing fix worked for the v sector. But u sector grows steadily: u peaks 2.39 -> 6.07 -> 12.79 over [0,1,2] -> blow-up at t=2.23. This is u-side Burgers shock formation: the explicit treatment of 3 u u_x lacks shock-capturing dissipation; once u steepens to a shock, dealiased spectral cannot represent the discontinuity and aliases burst back in. Bank kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass and kb-shallowWater-HLL-dam-break-pass recommend MUSCL/Godunov flux for the Burgers sector — that would be the E4 single-component move (which we cannot perform under the 3-iteration cap). Final saved array reaches T=8 only via blow-up padding; the clean physical window is t<=2.

Within the 3-iteration cap, the diagnosed remaining failure is u-side Burgers shock formation. The bank suggests MUSCL+Godunov as the next single-component move (kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass), but that would be E4 which is not permitted. We narrow the claim: the IMEX-CN+2/3-dealiasing recipe (E3) correctly handles the swept-KdV (v) sector but cannot survive the Burgers (u) sector beyond t~2.23. The final saved file contains blow-up signature in late snapshots. Final self-assessment: useful_self_assessment=false; not meeting the T=8 phenomenon target.
